title: Twins!!

title: Fixing “full path disclosure” issues

title: Election Day should be a holiday

title: Premortems
author: Matt Cutts
summary: Google has a pretty good culture of doing postmortems. When something fails, someone close to the failure tries to document what happened and why. title: Google Chrome Tips
author: Matt Cutts
summary: I started this blog post of Chrome tips in 2008. Even though this is a “hairball” post, some of these tips still work. – control-shift-V will paste your selection as plain text – control-shift-T will re-open the last tab you closed. You can repeat that to keep re-opening previously closed tabs. – Hover over a […]

title: How Cuil generates its categories
author: Matt Cutts
summary: This “hairball” post about Cuil isn’t really snarky, so I’ll post it. Cuil is no longer around, but it did spawn a funny post on Reddit about Cuil Theory. Cuil launched this week. For a search engineer, a new search engine is like a Christmas present: you can’t wait to play with it. Most search […]

title: Cheap internet-connected scale: Wii Balance Board + Linux
author: Matt Cutts
summary: You can ignore this ancient “hairball” blog post. Gather round, kids, and witness this blog post from a time *before internet-connected scales*. That’s right. Back then, we had to hack our Wii balance boards to connect them to the internet. Of course now you can buy wifi-connected scales from Fitbit and Withings. But in a […]

title: Wanted: bookmarks.html merging program
author: Matt Cutts
summary: You can ignore this “hairball” blog post. This post dates back to a time when people actually curated, saved, and managed their bookmarks.html file. Then Google Chrome introduced the ability to save and sync all your bookmarks, extensions, etc. in the cloud. Now I sign in to Chrome and everything is synced in the cloud. […]

title: TimeTrax gone, SXRecorder lives
author: Matt Cutts
summary: What’s that? You’ve never heard of an XMPCR? Don’t worry, the rest of the world hasn’t either. You can ignore this “hairball” post as I do spring cleaning on my blog. TimeTrax was a program that allowed XMPCR owners to listen to XM Radio on their computer. Even nicer, the program would “time shift” the […]

title: One million steps
author: Matt Cutts
summary: Note: this is a “hairball” post and you can ignore it. I was so proud of my first one million steps with a pre-Fitbit pedometer. Now I’ve done 13 million steps and it’s just kind of normal. For the record, the Fitbit is a great little pedometer, but I tend to lose one every few […]

title: Hack your iPhone: install a toolchain
author: Matt Cutts
summary: This “hairball” post is ancient and unfinished. Would anyone care about this now? Probably not. Maybe some future data archaeologist will care. In my previous post I covered what a toolchain is and why you need one to cross-compile applications for the iPhone. I’ve seen rumors that there will be a Windows toolchain soon, and […]

title: My blog needs to cough up a hairball
author: Matt Cutts
summary: My blog is almost eight years old, and I’ve published just under a thousand blog posts in that time. Along the way, I wrote about 100 draft notes that I never published. Sometimes I just didn’t finish the posts. Sometimes I thought they were too boring. Sometimes I wrote a blog post to debunk a […]

title: Malware warning
author: Matt Cutts
summary: Recently someone on twitter complained that Chrome was labeling their site as malware: http://Dvorak.org site blocked by Chrome browser after I wrote negative commentary about Google. I took a few minutes to compose a reply, so I’ll go ahead and post it here: Just to summarize: Chrome’s warning is correct. Your blog is hacked and […]

title: A big challenge: running a 50 mile race
author: Matt Cutts
summary: You may have heard of my 30 day challenges, where I try something new for 30 days. Those challenges are great to try out a new habit and see how you like it. But I’m also a big believer in picking out really big goals too. In 2010 I trained for a few months so […]

title: What I learned from time away from the internet and email
author: Matt Cutts
summary: Hey folks, I just finished January’s 30 day challenge: no news, no Twitter, fewer emails, and no social media in general. For February, my wife and I are trying a gluten-free, wheat-free month to see what that’s like. Okay, so how was January? I started with a week completely off the internet, which coincided with […]

title: Taking a week off from the internet
author: Matt Cutts
summary: The end of the year is a perfect time to think about goals. Did you get done what you wanted in 2012? What do you want to accomplish in 2013? Instead of setting year-long goals, I’m a big fan of trying out new things for a month at a time: This month I’m going to […]

title: PubCon 2012 slides: disavow links tool
author: Matt Cutts
summary: I spoke at PubCon 2012 last month in Las Vegas. A couple people have asked for my slides, so here they are: Several of the slides have links to additional information, in case you’re interested. We announced the disavow links tool during my session so that’s what a lot of the slides are about.

title: New 30 day challenge: get good sleep
author: Matt Cutts
summary: For October 2012, I tried to practice the ukulele every day. I ended up doing more traveling than I expected, but I managed to play ukulele most of the days. I’m still a total beginner, but it was a lot of fun! My favorite song to play so far is M.T.A. by the Kingston Trio. […]

title: My Halloween costume for 2012: Matt Romney!
author: Matt Cutts
summary: My fellow Americans, I’d like to introduce my latest Halloween costume: Matt Romney! My five-point plan for the Mitt Romney Halloween costume went like this: Start with a suit and tie. Put a little silver in my sideburns. My wife used some silver and white eye shadow. Swoosh the hair up a little bit and […]

title: Out for a few days: my grandfather is in the hospital
author: Matt Cutts
summary: My grandfather has been seriously ill this week, so I’m flying tonight to be with him in Tennessee. If you’re waiting on me for a reply about something, it will probably need to wait. I still hope to attend PubCon next week but I can’t promise that I’ll be able to make it. Added: My […]

title: My secret 30 day challenge and my new challenge: ukulele!
author: Matt Cutts
summary: Last month I did a secret 30 day challenge: everyday I did something nice for my wife. It could be surprising her with flowers, doing some chore around the house without her asking, or just trying to be present and focused when she wanted to talk. This challenge turned out really well. At first, I […]

title: Submit video topics and webmaster questions
author: Matt Cutts
summary: I’m planning to record some new webmaster questions next week. I made a Google Moderator page where you can submit topics and vote for different questions. Please ask your questions in on the Google Moderator page, not in the comments here. When the comments are in Moderator, people can vote them up and down.

title: “Why did our PageRank go down?”
author: Matt Cutts
summary: Recently a newspaper contacted me. Their PageRank had dropped from 7 to 3, and they wanted to know why. They genuinely didn’t seem know what the issue was, so I took some time to write them an in-depth reply. Part of the motivation for my blog is to provide information in more scalable ways, so […]

title: Please turn on two-factor authentication
author: Matt Cutts
summary: You should read Mat Honan’s heartbreaking tale of a hack attack and the ensuing discussion on Techmeme. Much of the story is about Amazon or Apple’s security practices, but I would still advise everyone to turn on Google’s two-factor authentication to make your Gmail account safer and less likely to get hacked. Two-factor authentication means […]

title: “Fetch as Googlebot” tool helps to debug hacked sites
author: Matt Cutts
summary: One of the most tenacious blackhat webspam techniques we continue to see is hacked sites. I wanted to remind site owners that our free “Fetch as Google” tool can be a really helpful way to see whether you’ve successfully cleaned up a hacked site. For example, recently a well-known musician’s website was hacked. The management […]

title: New 30 day challenge: No sending email after 9 p.m.
author: Matt Cutts
summary: Last month (June 2012), my 30 day challenge was to try to eat mindfully (eat more slowly, don’t eat while distracted by TV or web browsing, chew more, stop eating when I’m full, etc.). It turns out that eating mindfully is hard. I’m the sort of person that eats whatever is on my plate, so […]

title: Example email to a hacked site
author: Matt Cutts
summary: Beyond clear-cut blackhat webspam, the second-biggest category of spam that Google deals with is hacked sites. The most common reaction we hear from webmasters is “The problem is with the Google search. There is nothing wrong with our website.” That’s a real quote from an email one site owner recently sent us. Sadly, it turns […]

title: New 30 day challenge: No news
author: Matt Cutts
summary: I haven’t given an update on my 30 day challenges in, like, forever. So here goes: – In 2011, I paused my 30 day challenges to do a “six month challenge”: training to run a marathon. I ended up running the San Francisco marathon (while tweeting!) and a couple half-marathons. Pro tip: ramp up slowly […]

title: Eventful Events
author: Matt Cutts
summary: Updated: Okay, there’s a new plan. My wife is having surgery to put a screw in her foot, and the operation is two days before my panel. I really can’t leave her to come to SXSW. We’re going to try for me to Skype in to the panel instead. Cross your fingers. Every so often […]
